questions.q: In which year did World War One begin?
questions.explanation: Britain declared war on Germany on 4 August 1914, following Germany's invasion of neutral Belgium.

questions.q: What was the event in Sarajevo that triggered the war?
questions.a: The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand
questions.hint: An assassination of an heir to a throne
questions.explanation: Franz Ferdinand, heir to the Austro-Hungarian throne, was shot on 28 June 1914. The system of alliances pulled Europe's powers into the conflict that followed.

questions.q: What was the name given to the area between opposing trenches?
questions.explanation: No man's land was the deadly strip between front-line trenches — typically scarred by shell craters, barbed wire, and bodies.

questions.q: Which 1916 battle on the Western Front lasted nearly five months?
questions.explanation: The Somme (July–November 1916) saw over a million casualties combined. The first day alone cost the British army around 57,000 men.

questions.q: In which month and year did WW1 end?
questions.hint: The 11th hour of the 11th day of the 11th month
questions.explanation: The Armistice was signed on 11 November 1918. Remembrance Day commemorates the moment fighting stopped at 11am.
